pr.title: feat(travel): Phase 4 SEO + i18n 구현 — 여행지 페이지, sitemap, robots, GA, AI 언어 동기화

findings.title: Locale-dependent prompt branching lacks unit/integration coverage
findings.rationale: Prompt behavior now depends on optional locale and brittle string replacement. This is easy to regress (e.g., base prompt text edits silently break replacement) and the diff does not add tests for `ko/en/default` branches or request-to-prompt locale propagation.
findings.recommendation: Add unit tests for `buildTravelSystemPrompt` covering `ko`, `en`, and undefined locale. Add an API-level test that posts `locale` through `/api/chat` and asserts the generated system prompt variant.

findings.title: Locale routing behavior changed without middleware regression tests
findings.rationale: The diff introduces locale-enforcing middleware and a complex matcher regex, which is a high-impact routing surface. No accompanying middleware/integration test changes are present in the diff, so regressions in redirect/exclusion behavior are likely to go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add middleware integration tests that assert behavior for `/`, `/(ko|en)` paths, nested localized routes, and excluded paths (`/api`, `/_next`, static assets, `/login`). Include both redirect and pass-through expectations.

findings.title: Stored XSS risk from unescaped JSON-LD in destination detail page
findings.rationale: `name`, `description`, and `image` come from database content. Injecting `JSON.stringify(jsonLd)` directly into a `<script>` tag via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` does not escape `</script>` and related characters, so malicious content in destination fields can terminate the script block and execute attacker-controlled HTML/JS.
findings.recommendation: Serialize JSON-LD with HTML-safe escaping before injection (at minimum escape `<`, `>`, `&`, U+2028, U+2029), or use a trusted helper that guarantees script-safe JSON serialization. Keep lint guardrails enabled instead of bypassing `noDangerouslySetInnerHtml` for DB-backed data.

findings.title: Stored XSS risk from unescaped JSON-LD in destinations list page
findings.rationale: This page also embeds DB-derived fields (`nameKo/nameEn`, `slug`, `imageUrl`) into a raw JSON-LD `<script>` via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. If any stored value contains script-closing payloads, it can break out of the JSON-LD block and run arbitrary script in visitors’ browsers.
findings.recommendation: Apply the same script-safe JSON serialization fix here as well. Prefer a shared utility for JSON-LD emission that always escapes dangerous characters before rendering.

findings.title: Locale-specific landing CTA links to non-localized chat path
findings.rationale: The app routes chat under `/[locale]/chat`, but the CTA uses `/chat`. This forces locale re-detection in middleware and can navigate users to the wrong locale (e.g., leaving `/ko` and ending up in `/en/chat`).
findings.recommendation: Build the chat link with the active locale (e.g., `href={`/${locale}/chat`}`) so navigation preserves the current language deterministically.

findings.title: Sitemap generation introduces nondeterministic timestamps without test seam
findings.rationale: Using `new Date()` inside sitemap output makes results time-variant, which complicates stable assertions and tends to create flaky or brittle tests. The diff adds no tests around sitemap determinism or URL completeness across locales.
findings.recommendation: Inject a clock or derive stable timestamps for static routes, then add sitemap unit tests with mocked destination data to verify deterministic output and locale URL coverage.

findings.title: DestinationGrid state transitions are untested (search/filter/pagination coupling)
findings.rationale: The grid adds several coupled UI states (country filter, free-text search, pagination reset) that are prone to regression through future refactors. No component tests are added in the diff to lock expected behavior for these transitions.
findings.recommendation: Add component tests (RTL) covering: page reset on search/filter changes, empty result handling, pagination bounds, and locale-aware link rendering after filtering.

findings.title: Language switcher drops query parameters during locale change
findings.rationale: `usePathname()` excludes search params, so switching locale strips query state (e.g., context/filter params). This can break behavior tied to URL parameters after language toggle.
findings.recommendation: Preserve search params (and hash if needed) by combining `useSearchParams()` with the new localized pathname before `router.push`.

findings.title: Published destinations API silently truncates list to 30 by default
findings.rationale: The function is documented/used as the source for the destinations listing, but defaults to returning only 30 rows. As data grows, pages that call it without `limit` will never show destinations beyond the first 30, producing incomplete results and pagination over a truncated dataset.
findings.recommendation: Only apply `take` when `params.limit` is explicitly provided (or pass explicit pagination inputs from callers). Do not hard-cap default list retrieval to 30 if full listing is intended.

findings.title: New destination seeding path added without idempotency or data-shape tests
findings.rationale: The diff introduces a new seed path and multilingual JSON shaping that multiple pages depend on, but there are no tests in the diff validating idempotent upsert behavior, published filtering assumptions, or JSON field structure expectations.
findings.recommendation: Add Prisma integration tests for seed idempotency and field shape (`description/highlights` ko/en keys), plus service-level tests confirming only `published=true` records are returned.
